pending.body:
finding_summary: `build_mock_bv_adapter()` seeds only one `--robot-next` response in `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:331-336`. Because `MockBvAdapter` consumes queued responses and then panics when the queue is empty (`src/test_support/bv.rs:163-169`), a second `robot-next` call against the same fixture fails even though the workspace state is unchanged.
affected_files: src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs; src/test_support/bv.rs
remediation_hint: Make the BV fixture repeatable, either by adding dispatch-based matching or by preloading enough identical responses for repeated `robot-next` reads.

finding_summary: The synthetic `br create` path always returns the hard-coded id `ms-e2e-scenario.task-follow-up-validation` (`src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:36,339-374,490-503`). If code under test creates two follow-up beads or retries the create path after a partial failure, the fixture aliases both operations to the same bead instead of allocating a fresh id, which does not match real `br create` behavior.
affected_files: src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs
remediation_hint: Track a create counter and generate unique ids per created bead, or reject duplicate creates explicitly so tests cannot silently pass against aliased state.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 6 -> Plan and Implement
